name: Artificial Epanorthosis: Why large language models overuse a classical rhetorical figure, and how to mitigate it
summary: arXiv:2607.21498v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: A rhetorical figure that Cicero and Quintilian catalogued two thousand years ago reappears, systematically, in the text of large language models: e...
description: arXiv:2607.21498v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: A rhetorical figure that Cicero and Quintilian catalogued two thousand years ago reappears, systematically, in the text of large language models: epanorthosis, the self-correction of the specimen {\guillemotleft}This is not a course. It is a journey of transformation{\guillemotright}. This essay argues that the overuse is a trained disposition,...
author: Federico Boggia
